title: Conflict Stages, Semantic Resolution, and Rerere · Recovery Case: Gita Conflict Taxonomy
summary: A repeated long-running integration conflicts across generated code, renamed policy files, and tests; previous manual resolutions are inconsistent and one clean textual result changes behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita conflict taxonomy increment in three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish content, add/add, delete/modify, rename, directory/file, binary, mode, submodule, and recursive conflicts and select evidence for each.
Inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Conflict Taxonomy
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G77 is a theory in a disposable repository. the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict. The learner must predict stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction, run git ls-files --unmerged, explain all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context, test this changed condition: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Conflict Taxonomy contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish content, add/add, delete/modify, rename, directory/file, binary, mode, submodule, and recursive conflicts and select evidence for each.

Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G77 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict. The learner must predict stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction, run git ls-files --unmerged, explain all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context, test this changed condition: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer. A defensible solution applies Gita Conflict Taxonomy, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 4
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict
git ls-files --unmerged
# expected: stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction
# observation: all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context
# changed-case: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer
# transfer-limit: rerere output remains a proposal and must pass semantic changed-case verification
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Every conflict can be completely diagnosed by reading only textual conflict markers. This fails the Gita Conflict Taxonomy contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Build Gita Conflict Taxonomy evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-conflict-taxonomy-14k5bo" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Conflict Taxonomy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-conflict-taxonomy-14k5bo
# scenario-key: trace-14k5bo probe-ob5k41 boundary-k5bo14 owner-14k5box fault-14k5boz
# before-ref: main=a11ce14 candidate=b00b1bo
# hypothesis: the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict
git ls-files --unmerged
# expected: stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction
# observation: all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context
# changed-case: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail
# after-ref: main=a11ce14 candidate=c0ffeeo
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer
# transfer-limit: rerere output remains a proposal and must pass semantic changed-case verification

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish content, add/add, delete/modify, rename, directory/file, binary, mode, submodule, and recursive conflicts and select evidence for each.
